always.exam: Fracture/trauma screen (deformity, bony tenderness, ability to move fingers) before further testing if any trauma mechanism
always.why: A FOOSH or direct trauma mechanism requires excluding a fracture (distal radius, scaphoid) or dislocation before proceeding with provocative special tests.
always.confirmsOrExcludes: Screens for occult fracture/dislocation requiring imaging referral

always.exam: Active + passive wrist ROM (flexion, extension, radial deviation, ulnar deviation) with end-feel
always.why: Establishes the mobility baseline and end-feel pattern that differentiates capsular/OA restriction from soft-tissue/ligamentous presentations.

always.exam: Neurological screen (median/ulnar/radial nerve distribution, Phalen's, Tinel's at wrist)
always.why: Neurological symptoms are common at the wrist/hand and a structured screen differentiates carpal tunnel syndrome from cervical referral or other nerve entrapment.
always.confirmsOrExcludes: Screens carpal tunnel syndrome vs other neurological sources

add.why: Radial-sided wrist pain is the classic presentation of De Quervain's tenosynovitis and requires this specific confirmatory test.

add.exam: TFCC stress test + ECU synergy test + ulnar-sided palpation
add.why: Ulnar-sided wrist pain has a distinct differential (TFCC tear vs ECU tendinopathy/instability) that requires dedicated testing to separate.
add.confirmsOrExcludes: Differentiates TFCC tear from ECU tendinopathy/instability

add.exam: Anatomical snuffbox palpation + Watson scaphoid shift test
add.why: A FOOSH mechanism requires screening for scaphoid fracture (easily missed and high false-negative rate on initial x-ray) and scapholunate ligament instability.
add.confirmsOrExcludes: Confirms/excludes scaphoid fracture and scapholunate instability

add.exam: Deformity/swelling observation and urgent imaging referral before any further provocative testing
add.why: A suspected distal radius fracture must be confirmed or excluded before any loading or manual testing is attempted.
Thumb base pain in an appropriate age group is the classic presentation of 1st CMC osteoarthritis and requires this specific confirmatory test.

add.exam: Palpation for flexor tendon nodule/catching at the A1 pulley with active finger flexion/extension
add.why: A catching/locking digit pattern needs direct confirmation of a flexor tendon nodule to distinguish trigger finger from other hand pathology.

add.exam: Bilateral neurological screen + systemic/metabolic history review (thyroid, diabetes, pregnancy, rheumatoid arthritis)
add.why: Bilateral carpal tunnel symptoms raise suspicion for a systemic contributing cause that should be screened rather than treated as a purely local mechanical problem.
add.confirmsOrExcludes: Screens for a systemic cause of bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome

supporting.exam: Resisted testing (wrist extension, wrist flexion, thumb extension/abduction, finger flexion/extension) for strength and pain-on-resist
supporting.why: Differentiates contractile (tendon/muscle) sources from inert (ligament/capsule) sources per Cyriax selective tissue tension principles.
supporting.confirmsOrExcludes: Localises contractile vs inert tissue involvement

supporting.exam: Grip strength measurement (dynamometer) and intrinsic vs extrinsic hand muscle screen
supporting.why: Quantifies functional strength baseline and helps differentiate neurological weakness from disuse or local tendon pathology.
supporting.confirmsOrExcludes: Baseline grip strength and intrinsic/extrinsic balance

supporting.exam: Functional + outcome measure (DASH/Quick-DASH or PRWE, PSFS top 3 activities)
supporting.why: Quantifies baseline wrist/hand-related disability for goal-setting and re-assessment.

supporting.exam: Imaging (only if fracture suspected, red flag present, or failed conservative management)
supporting.why: Imaging is not routine for straightforward soft-tissue wrist/hand overuse presentations; reserve for fracture screening or non-resolving presentations.
supporting.confirmsOrExcludes: Confirms structural pathology when clinically indicated
